description_long: This is a weathered caution sign with a diamond-shaped face mounted atop a slender, rusted pole. The sign's surface is heavily corroded, displaying a dark brown patina with patches of lighter rust and some faint remnants of its original markings. The front face appears to have a faded triangular warning symbol, though the details are obscured by rust and age. The back of the sign is similarly corroded, with no visible markings or text. The pole is thin and straight, showing consistent rust and wear along its length, suggesting prolonged outdoor exposure. The overall appearance indicates it is an old, metal caution sign typically found in urban or roadside environments.
description: A heavily rusted, diamond-shaped caution sign on a thin, corroded pole with faint, barely visible warning markings. The entire object shows significant weathering and age.
description_view_0: Front view showing the diamond-shaped sign face with faint, rust-obscured warning symbol and extensive corrosion.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of both the sign and pole, with visible rust texture.
description_view_2: Rear view of the sign, showing a plain, rusted diamond face with no visible markings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin, rusted profile of the sign and pole.
